title: The 2026 Grammys in Review - Irish Rover
text:
Song of the Year Analysis: Billie Eilish's "Wildflower"

The Winner: Billie Eilish claimed Song of the Year for "Wildflower," her 2024 track created with brother Finneas. This victory marked a notable upset, as the song wasn't considered a frontrunner heading into the ceremony.

Key Commentary: The win demonstrated surprising strength at the awards. Eilish and Finneas defeated formidable competition, including Sabrina Carpenter's "Manchild" and "Golden" by K-pop Demon Hunters, which had enjoyed significant commercial momentum.

Significance: This represents Eilish's third Song of the Year Grammy, underscoring her consistent excellence in songwriting. The recognition reinforces her status as one of contemporary music's most celebrated creative voices, particularly in collaboration with her brother's production expertise.

The victory highlighted how Grammy voters sometimes honor artistic merit over chart dominance, selecting a track that, while not universally predicted to win, resonated sufficiently with the Recording Academy's membership to secure the award.
